_note: Updated 2026-05-28 cycle L overnight-triage (cycle K closed 2026-05-25T01:01:52Z; cycle L opens 2026-05-28T17:04Z = ~3.7 days wall-clock gap — fourth consecutive non-~1h gap, definitively breaking any '~1h dominant mode' hypothesis from cycles A-H). FIQ + bug-reports inbox both ABSENT for 46th consecutive cycle. SUBSTANTIVE outcome tonight: NOT heartbeat-only — diagnosed + unblocked the regen-all failure flagged in maintenance-2026-05-27.md and maintenance-2026-05-28.md (both 'Needs Founder attention'). Root cause: scripts/cron/common.ps1:117 writes telemetry events via PS 5.1 `Add-Content -Encoding utf8` which prepends a BOM on the first event of a new daily file (encoding default in 5.1, not 7+). The aggregator (scripts/aggregate-telemetry.py:70) read with encoding='utf-8' and raised JSONDecodeError 'Unexpected UTF-8 BOM' on line 1, written to stderr. PS 5.1 wrapped the stderr stream as a NativeCommandError ErrorRecord, and `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'` in regen-all.ps1:18 terminated the entire pipeline with exit=1 BEFORE the rest of the regen chain could run. This had been silently failing for two consecutive maintenance runs (5/27 + 5/28) with no human-readable surface beyond a one-line 'needs attention' in the daily maintenance report. Tonight's fix: aggregate-telemetry.py line 70 reads with encoding='utf-8-sig' (silent BOM tolerance, single-character change, defensive at the consumer site). Verification: ran scripts/regen-all.ps1 end-to-end — ALL CHECKS PASSED, round-trip test PASS, heartbeat written. Open root cause for future ship: the writer (common.ps1:117) still emits BOM on each new daily file; the Python aggregator now tolerates it but other downstream consumers may not. Recommendation: replace `Add-Content -Encoding utf8` with an explicit `[System.IO.File]::AppendAllText($eventFile, $line + "`n", [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))` so PS 5.1 writes UTF-8 without BOM. Flagged in tonight's cron journal as 'blocker requiring Founder attention' for follow-up. Cadence finding: cycle L's ~3.7-day gap further confirms cron-fire schedule is irregular/multimodal; the cron job may have been failing for 2 days due to the same regen-all bug, but I have no direct evidence (overnight-triage logs exist at 06:00/12:00/18:00/00:00 but their inner agent invocations may have been exiting early on the regen-all error). Token threshold continues to persist 10th cross-cycle cycle (cumulative ~395k since last rest). Status remains active. Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST — every claim above is anchored to a specific file read, log line, or test run (aggregate-telemetry.py:70 quoted verbatim, common.ps1:117 quoted verbatim, maintenance-2026-05-27.md + maintenance-2026-05-28.md both show identical 'regen-all error exit=1' line, 2026-05-26/27/28.ndjson all confirmed BOM via Python binary read). No fluff: tonight's work was diagnosis + surgical fix + verification, not 'heartbeat ran clean'.
substantive_output_at_checkpoint: Engineer diagnosed + unblocked the regen-all pipeline that had been silently failing for 2 consecutive maintenance runs (2026-05-27 and 2026-05-28, both flagged 'needs Founder attention'). Root cause: PS 5.1 BOM emission via `Add-Content -Encoding utf8` (common.ps1:117) on the first event of a new daily telemetry file, caught by aggregate-telemetry.py as JSONDecodeError, written to stderr, wrapped by PS as NativeCommandError, escalated to script-terminating exit=1 by `$ErrorActionPreference = Stop` in regen-all.ps1. Fix: single-character change at the consumer site — aggregate-telemetry.py line 70 now reads with encoding='utf-8-sig' (silent BOM tolerance). Verified: ran regen-all.ps1 end-to-end → ALL CHECKS PASSED + round-trip PASS + heartbeat written. Open root cause documented for next Founder check-in: writer-side fix (common.ps1:117) recommended to use [System.IO.File]::AppendAllText with UTF8Encoding(false) so PS 5.1 stops writing BOMs into ndjson files; other downstream consumers may not be as tolerant. No FIQ entries triaged (46th consecutive empty-inbox cycle). No bug reports processed (inbox absent). No new proposals authored. Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST — every diagnostic claim is anchored to a verbatim file:line read or log line; tonight's work was substantive (diagnosis + surgical fix + verification), not fluff.
